chat.title: Messages
chat.hint: Enter a Growboard member’s email — after verification, you both appear in each other’s contact lists. Login = email. Online status updates in real time while this page is open.
chat.userNotFound: No user with this email was found.

auth.sessionExpired: Your session has expired or the token is no longer valid. Sign in again to load data.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcome: Welcome to the Team Maturity Assessment System!
dashboard.description: Here you can manage teams, conduct assessments, and analyze results.
dashboard.noAssessmentYet: No completed assessment yet — take the survey to see your score and chart.
dashboard.confirmDeleteTeam: Delete this team? This cannot be undone.
dashboard.passSurveyFirst: Please complete an assessment for your team first.

survey.title: Team Survey
survey.question: Question
survey.teamExists: Team with this name already exists

newSurvey.selectTeamForMaturity: Select a team to assess maturity
newSurvey.disclaimer: Important: answer options go from lower maturity (1) to higher maturity (5). Choosing a higher level implies previous practices are already in place.
newSurvey.progressHint: Question progress — click a step to go back

maturity.title: Maturity Assessment by Link
maturity.surveyLangHint: Language of the question text. By default it matches the manager’s survey language; switch if you need the other language.
maturity.alreadyCompleted: This assessment has already been completed.
maturity.teamSelfThanks: Thank you — your response is saved. Others can use the same link to respond.
maturity.withTeamSelfLink: Add a team self-assessment link (multiple people, one link, no login)
maturity.needLoginTeamLink: Sign in to enable the team self-assessment link.
maturity.teamSelfLinkEnableHint: The manager survey is done. Create a separate link so team members can take the same questionnaire without signing in (responses are averaged on the chart with the manager’s scores).
maturity.teamSelfLinkShareHint: Share this link with the team. Each submission is saved separately.
maturity.teamSelfLinkLocked: The team link was created by another user. Only they can see the comparison on the chart.
maturity.teamSelfLinkLoadError: Could not load team link status.
maturity.pdfError: Error generating PDF. Please try again.
maturity.linkCreated: Link created. Share it with your team.
maturity.commentPlaceholder: E.g. context, examples, why you chose this answer…

results.dontKnowTitle: What to do next quarter (“Don’t know” answers)
results.dontKnowHint: Practical steps so the team can clarify whether they have this in place.

edit.title: Change answers
edit.hint: Update the answers you need, then click “Save and recalculate report”.
edit.commentPlaceholder: Context, examples, notes on the answer…
edit.notCompleted: The assessment is not completed yet. Finish the survey first.

surveys.title: Surveys
surveys.pageSubtitle: Create surveys, manage templates, and copy invitation text for email quickly.

wizard.introLead: Pick e-NPS for a team or 360° for feedback about one person. Then add a title, optional links to a team or employee, preview questions, and share the link.
wizard.introStep1Body: e-NPS measures team sentiment and engagement. 360° is anonymous peer feedback about one employee.
wizard.introStep2Body: Give the survey a clear name. Link it to a team or the person being reviewed to filter reports.
wizard.introStep3Body: Default questions are used out of the box. You can edit them, save your template, or draft with AI — then preview and copy the link.
wizard.quickBody: Standard questions for the chosen type. Fewer steps: type, title, optional links — then preview and link.
wizard.customBody: Pick a saved template, use the editor, save a copy, or an AI draft — when you need to change wording before launch.
wizard.quickTemplateHint: Standard questions are used. Need your own wording or AI?
wizard.landingTypeHint: Click a type to open the wizard, or use the button below.

surveys.invitePasteHint: Paste the text into email or a messenger and send it to participants.
surveys.confirmDeleteTemplateNamed: Delete template "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
surveys.templateDeleteNotFound: Template not found or you do not have permission.

hr.pageLead: For HR: team e-NPS or 360° feedback about one employee. Steps below take you from type selection to the participant link.
hr.enpsShort: Engagement and employee experience for a team or unit.
enpsWhenBullets: Use to measure e-NPS and team sentiment.
Questions focus on overall work experience, not one person.
Optionally link the survey to a team for reporting.
hr.feedback360Short: Anonymous peer feedback about a specific employee (competencies, examples).
feedback360WhenBullets: Use when several respondents assess one person.
Often used before reviews, rotation, or development plans.
Turn on the link below and select the person being reviewed.
hr.introScenario: You start from the standard question set or a saved template. Edit wording in the editor if needed. To keep changes, save a copy under My templates or save from the editor. Then click Create survey — you will see a preview, then get a shareable link.
hr.aiPopover1: Describe the goal — the model will suggest questions.
hr.aiPopover2: The draft opens in the editor; you can edit everything and save as your template.
hr.optionalBlockLead: Links help filter reports and context; participants still use the same survey link.
hr.teamLinkHint: For e-NPS: segment responses by team in analytics.
hr.employeeLinkHint: For 360°: choose the employee the feedback is about (needed for meaningful reports).
hr.glossary: Standard template is the built-in set for the chosen type. The dropdown lists your saved versions. Open editor — change questions before launch. Save copy of template — duplicate the current set under a new name for future runs. Generate with AI — draft from your description, then edit in the editor.
hr.cardEditorBody: Edit wording and question types. If no template is selected, the standard set for this type is loaded.
hr.cardCopyBody: Saves a copy of the current set (selected or standard) under a new name for reuse.
hr.cardAiBody: Briefly describe the goal — get suggested questions and open them in the editor.
hr.aiModalLead: Describe goal, audience, and constraints (anonymity, deadline, focus). You can change all questions in the editor and save as your template.
hr.aiBriefPlaceholder: Example: e-NPS for product X IT team, remote-work focus; or 360° for a team lead, focus on communication and leadership…

preview.title: Survey preview
preview.editHint: To change wording, click Edit questions. After editing in the template editor, save the template (Save in the editor) so you do not lose your version for future surveys.

templateEditor.saveHint: Saving the template stores your question set for future surveys.
templateEditor.unsavedCloseConfirm: Questions were changed. Save as a template to reuse next time? Press Cancel, then Save. Close without saving?

surveyResultsPage.textQ8: What do you like most about working at our company?
surveyResultsPage.textQ9: What would you improve at our company?

assessmentResults.authNotice: Your results are not saved. Sign up to keep them.
assessmentResults.errProgress: Not enough data to show progress. Complete at least one assessment.
assessmentResults.errPassFirst: Please complete an assessment for your team first.
assessmentResults.alertNeedPlan: Generate and save a plan before requesting recommendations.
assessmentResults.marketModalP1: This metric is based on data from more than 100 organizations and is refreshed quarterly as teams complete assessments on the GrowBoard platform.
assessmentResults.marketModalP2: It includes teams using the platform and consulting partners in the CIS region.
assessmentResults.marketModalP3: It reflects average values for key IT team effectiveness metrics so you can compare your team’s level to the broader market trend.

systemThinking.title: Systems thinking iceberg
systemThinking.introLead: The iceberg helps move from a single visible symptom to causes: recurring patterns, system rules, beliefs, and the experience behind them — so you find lasting leverage, not only quick fixes.
systemThinking.createLead: Start by describing the event — what is visible on the surface. You can fill levels in any order afterwards.
systemThinking.createAiPopover1: Each level has one answer: type it in the field below.
systemThinking.createAiPopover2: If it is hard to phrase, write “don’t know” in the answer and tap “AI help”.
systemThinking.createAiPopover3: Progress is saved when you switch levels or close the window.
systemThinking.scenariosTitle: Common situations — tap to paste into the Event field:
systemThinking.workAiPopover1: Use “AI help” to get wording ideas for the current level.
systemThinking.workAiPopover2: If you are stuck, type “don’t know” in the answer field.
systemThinking.workAiPopover3: You can switch levels in any order; the draft saves automatically.
systemThinking.genHint: Available when all five levels are filled.
systemThinking.alertPdfError: Could not build PDF. Try printing from the browser (Ctrl+P).

event.title: Event
event.question: Describe a concrete event or symptom: what happened, who was involved, when did you notice?
event.description: Example: “The day before release, delivery was postponed again because of overnight bugs.”

pattern.title: Behavior pattern
pattern.question: What recurring pattern or regularity sits behind this?
pattern.description: Example: “For the third sprint in a row we miss demo because tasks were not tested enough.”

system_structure.title: System structure
system_structure.question: What structures, processes, rules, or procedures in the system keep this pattern in place?
system_structure.description: Example: “No dedicated regression slot; acceptance is only the developer checklist.”

mental_model.title: Mental model
mental_model.question: What beliefs or assumptions support this structure?
mental_model.description: Example: “Testing always slows us down; better to ship fast and fix in production.”

experience.title: Experience
experience.question: What past experience could have shaped these beliefs?
experience.description: Example: “A year ago a hard deadline slipped because testing took long — since then it is underestimated.”

deadlines.label: Missed deadlines
deadlines.text: The team regularly misses release dates: the deadline moves at the last minute, stakeholders are unhappy, developers work overtime.

meetings.label: Meeting conflicts
meetings.text: Daily syncs repeat the same arguments: who was supposed to do what and why tasks are still “stuck”.

quality.label: Quality and bugs
quality.text: After releases users report similar bugs; fixes ship as urgent patches, but the issue comes back.

projectCard.title: Project management map
projectCard.projectNamePh: Project name (e.g. opening a new branch)
projectCard.section1Hint: Up to 10 tasks. Click the traffic light for status.
projectCard.section2Hint: MUST — critical, SHOULD — important, NICE — nice to have.
projectCard.section3Hint: Describe in words what delivery depends on (like in a Scrum discussion): teams, vendors, approvals, infrastructure.
projectCard.dependenciesPlaceholder: E.g. Waiting on Legal for the contract; billing integration blocks release; IT must provision prod access…
projectCard.aiFailed: Could not get an AI response. Check limits or configuration.
projectCard.linkHint: Click the target stage to connect
projectCard.linksLabel: Links (or click two stages on the diagram):
projectCard.section4Hint: Elements limiting overall progress. You can list several.
projectCard.bottleneckDescPh: Why it is a bottleneck: causes, impact
projectCard.section6Hint: Issues that need a decision now.
projectCard.exportPdfError: Could not generate PDF. Please try again.

motivation.title: Employee Motivation Analysis
motivation.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this employee?
motivation.icebergModalLead: Like the systems-thinking iceberg: context above the line, what you can see at the surface, and deeper patterns underneath. Describe what you notice — AI will infer DISC and motivation levers.
motivation.tierVisible: Above the surface — what shows up under pressure and with people
motivation.tierDeep: Below the surface — work style and reactions to change

conflicts.title: Conflict Management
conflicts.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this conflict?

disc.title: DISC Assessment
disc.description: Take the DISC assessment to determine your personality type
disc.introBullet1: Determine your personality type using the DISC model
disc.introBullet2: Understand your strengths as a manager
disc.introBullet3: Get recommendations for developing leadership skills
disc.question: Question

common.teamCreateFailed: Could not create the team. The name may already be taken.

register.success: Registration successful! You can now log in.
register.beforeRegisterInviteHint:
Need an invite code? Email the administrator at artjoms.grinakins@gmail.com to request access.

Continue with registration?

profile.title: My Profile
profile.discDescription: Determine your personality type using the DISC methodology
profile.jwtHint: Use this token in Postman as a Bearer header. Do not share it with anyone.
profile.jwtMissing: No token found. Please sign in again.

meetingDesign.title: Meeting Design
meetingDesign.noDesigns: You don't have any meeting designs yet
meetingDesign.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this meeting design?

icebergIntro.title: How to use meeting design
icebergIntro.lead: Clarify the goal and constraints first, then generate a timed agenda. You can edit blocks afterward and ask the AI for facilitation tips.
icebergIntro.step1Body: Title and goal describe what participants should leave with. The clearer they are, the better the AI can pick activities.
icebergIntro.step2Body: Use checkboxes for format (remote/in-person), group size, and style. Add free-text constraints for extra rules.
icebergIntro.step3Body: Review blocks after generation, regenerate a single block if needed, or open the facilitator AI helper for live questions.

formAssist.lead: Describe the meeting in one or two sentences — the AI will draft title, goal, and constraint notes. Edit before generating the full agenda.
formAssist.placeholder: E.g. retro after a missed release, 1h, 8 people, remote…

fieldHints.title: Short working title; shown in your design list.
fieldHints.goal: Observable or measurable outcome: what changes after the meeting.
fieldHints.duration: Generated block durations will add up to this total.
fieldHints.team: Optional link to a team for future filtering.
fieldHints.constraints: Checkboxes cover common constraints; add specifics below.

ai.panelSubtitle: Set the meeting focus and response language — the AI will suggest short topic lines for discussion.
ai.themePlaceholder: E.g. post-mortem after a failed release, onboarding a new lead…
ai.viewAiHint: Text-only hints (no voice). Pick context and optionally ask a question.
ai.questionPlaceholder: E.g. how to engage quiet participants?
ai.noTopics: Topics will appear here after you request them.
ai.loginRequired: Sign in to use AI (session expired or missing token).

home.title: Assess Your Agile Team's Maturity
home.description: Take the survey and get personalized recommendations to improve your team.

features.maturityDescription: Get AI-powered recommendations for improving team dynamics, roles, and processes. Track progress over time and set conscious goals for team growth using our platform.
features.discDescription: GrowBoard helps you understand what truly motivates your employees. Get personalized recommendations and brief profiles for each team member — all within one platform.
features.conflictDescription: Identify team conflicts and get AI recommendations for resolution directly in the app — based on proven strategies and employee behavior analysis.

newHome.kicker: A platform to improve teams and products
newHome.title: Make delivery predictable — with GrowBoard
newHome.subtitle: Assess maturity, spot bottlenecks, and get actionable recommendations — so you ship value faster with less management noise.
newHome.trustedSubtitle: International brands that use practices aligned with what our tools support.

areas.smTitle: For Scrum Masters / Team Leads / Facilitators
areas.smDesc: Facilitation, conflicts, communication quality, and process improvement.
areas.pmDesc: Visibility, risk management, and delivery predictability.
areas.poDesc: Value focus, better requirements, and collaboration with the team.
areas.bizDesc: Understand delivery issues and growth opportunities.

feedback.title: What people say
feedback.lead: Short notes from teams and leaders using GrowBoard.

a.quote: Maturity surveys by link removed our coordination pain—people take the survey and we see trends in one place.
a.author: Helena K.

b.quote: The agile practice library and meeting design tools save time explaining what a good retro looks like to new facilitators.
b.author: Michael T.

c.quote: The dashboard and usability views help show the business where we lose speed—without extra slide decks.
c.author: Anna R.

about.title: About us
about.p1: GrowBoard is a platform for mature teams and product organizations: surveys, maturity assessment, facilitation tools, and analytics in one place.
about.p2: We combine proven agile practices with clear flows for Scrum Masters, product owners, PMs, and business stakeholders—so you spend less time debating process and more time shipping value.
about.p3: The product evolves with our users: we ship features where pain is real—from backlog prep to delivery transparency.

security.title: Security
security.p1: Access to workspace data is protected by authentication; sensitive actions require a signed-in account.
security.p2: Use HTTPS in production; passwords and tokens are not shown in the UI.
security.p3: AI features send the minimum context needed for the task; for full data-processing details, check with your org admin and the privacy policy.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.p1: Core flows (home, many tools and guides) are available to registered users under the current product model.
pricing.p2: Advanced capabilities (team surveys, integrations, enterprise reporting) may be offered separately—contact GrowBoard for up-to-date plans.
pricing.p3: Special terms may apply for education and non-profits—reach out via the site or in-product contacts.

businessValue.title: Quick business value assessment
businessValue.lead: Pick factors from the pool, rate each 1–5, and get a total score with a priority hint. Add backlog items manually, from pasted text, or extract them via AI from text or a screenshot.
businessValue.formulaHint: Score = sum of “+” factors minus sum of “−” factors (e.g. profit + urgency + regulatory − delivery risk). Bands: 12+ do now, 7–11 plan this quarter, ≤6 defer/reject.
businessValue.factorsHint: Choose which criteria count. “−” means a higher rating increases the penalty in the formula (risk).
businessValue.scaleLegend: For each criterion use 1–5: 1 — weaker according to the hints below, 5 — stronger. Values in between are gradual.
businessValue.pastePlaceholder: One line per item, or paste a block and use “Parse with AI”.
businessValue.addLines: Add each line as an item
businessValue.emptyState: Add items: paste one per line, parse a text block, or upload a screenshot.
businessValue.copyFallback: Could not copy automatically — use CSV/TXT export instead.

agileKata.pageLead: Scientific thinking for teams: challenge → current → target → small experiments → learning. Not detailed planning — fast cycles and evidence.
agileKata.formsHint: Each form is a separate canvas (e.g. different challenges or teams). Create several, switch between them, and save each one.
agileKata.formsEmpty: No saved forms yet — create one with «New canvas» or load the example.
agileKata.gapBody: Target: «{target}». Current: «{current}». Use experiments to close the gap in 1–3 day steps.
agileKata.gapNeedCurrent: You have a target — add measurable current condition (numbers/facts).
agileKata.gapNeedTarget: You have current metrics — define a measurable target outcome.

challenge.title: Challenge
challenge.hint: Long-term direction, business context, and one clear metric (avoid vague “improve” or “faster”).

target.title: Target condition
target.hint: Expected outcome in numbers and conditions that must be true.

current.title: Current condition
current.hint: Facts and observable measures — not opinions.

experiments.title: Experiments
experiments.hint: Small steps (1–3 days): action, hypothesis, result, learning.
experiments.empty: No experiments yet — add one when you have a next step.
experiments.impactedCurrent: This experiment affected the current condition
experiments.impactedHint: Reflect on the outcome: did the step move the measurable current state toward the target?

obstacles.title: Obstacles
obstacles.hint: List blockers. Select the one you are working on now.

ai.title: AI assistant & mentor
ai.lead: Three modes: sharpen wording and metrics, guided questions, or experiment coaching.
ai.descHelper: Finds vague text, suggests measurable phrasing and concrete edits.
ai.descMentor: Asks the next best question in the Mentor–Improver cycle based on your canvas.
ai.descCoach: Checks hypotheses, size of experiments, facts vs assumptions, and recorded learning.
ai.messagePh: e.g. “Help me phrase the metric” or answer the mentor…

learn.whatP1: Scientific thinking: form a hypothesis, run a small experiment, compare result to expectation, learn and adjust.
learn.whatP2: Progress through experiments, not big upfront plans.
learn.whatP3: Continuous learning: each cycle improves how you see the system.
learn.whatP4: Unlike classic planning, the focus is on the next safe-to-try step and verifiable learning.
learn.step2: Describe Current condition with facts and numbers.
learn.step4: Run small experiments; record hypothesis, result, learning.
learn.step5: List obstacles, pick the current one, repeat the cycle.

agileTools.title: Agile tools
agileTools.lead: A compact library of practices: what each is and what it gives your team or product. Search filters names and descriptions.
agileTools.askAi: Ask AI for a deeper guide
agileTools.aiFollowPh: e.g. How do we run this in a remote team of 8?
agileTools.aiNeedAuth: Sign in again to use AI.
agileTools.aiDisclaimer: Generated by AI; verify critical details for your context.

newUi.dashboardSubtitle: Your teams, surveys, and quick links in one place.
newUi.dashboardInterviewBanner: Practice a technical interview with AI (role, level, optional job description).
newUi.maturitySubtitle: Create a link and share it so people can complete the assessment.

reportInsights.title: AI report analyzer
reportInsights.subtitle: Upload a report — get the spots that deserve attention
reportInsights.lead: A tool for owners and leaders: upload any report — IT, finance, operations or HR — and the AI returns a short digest, highlights suspicious numbers, discrepancies and growth opportunities.
reportInsights.bullet1: Supports HTML dashboards, PDF, images and plain text.
reportInsights.bullet2: AI looks for anomalies: total mismatches, sudden swings, outliers and weak trends.
reportInsights.bullet3: You get a traffic-light health grade, a list of insights and questions to ask the team.
reportInsights.uploadHint: File up to 6 MB. Or paste the key numbers and comments below.
reportInsights.dropzonePlaceholder: Choose a file (HTML, PDF, PNG/JPG, TXT, CSV)
reportInsights.pastePlaceholder: For example: 'Revenue $12.4M, plan $12.0M, margin 18% (Q2: 22%)…' — the more numbers and context, the better.
reportInsights.notesPlaceholder: For example: 'margin drop and contractor spend felt off'. AI will weigh these cues.

errors.fileTooLarge: File is larger than 6 MB — please upload a smaller file.
errors.generic: Analysis failed. Try again or use a smaller file.

results.noSummary: AI could not produce a summary.

hub.title: Agile Training
hub.subtitle: Interactive exercises on Agile, Scrum and Kanban for groups of 10–30 people
hub.lead: Create a training, hand unique links to each team and watch aggregated answers in real time. Participants don't need to sign in.

principles.title: Agile principles — swipe trainer
principles.desc: Teams swipe 12 Agile Manifesto principles: 'relevant' vs 'outdated'. After each answer the group stats and a provocative comment appear.
principles.bullet2: No sign-in, runs in the browser

scrum.title: Scrum myths and truth
scrum.desc: Cards with Scrum statements; participants vote 'myth' or 'truth' and you debrief together.

kanban.title: WIP limits in Kanban
kanban.desc: A flow simulation: teams pick WIP limits and see how lead time and throughput change.

cynefin.title: Cynefin — where does this problem belong?
cynefin.desc: 8 universal cases (government, manufacturing, education, crises). Participants assign each case to a Cynefin domain, pick an action strategy and then see the expert answer and the group's view.
cynefin.bullet2: Drag & drop or click on a zone
cynefin.bullet3: Consequences of each wrong choice are discussed

iceberg.title: Systems thinking — Iceberg model
iceberg.desc: Participants unpack real situations across the iceberg levels: events, patterns, structures, mental models. Then they design interventions and compare with their group.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1: The facilitator creates a training session and adds several groups (teams).
howItWorks.step2: Each group gets a unique link like /g/team-xyz.
howItWorks.step3: Participants open the link on their own devices — no sign-up required.
howItWorks.step4: The facilitator sees progress, the most controversial principles and how groups differ.

facilitator.title: Agile principles — facilitator panel
facilitator.lead: Create a training, add groups and share links. Answers are collected per team.
facilitator.noSessions: No trainings yet. Create your first one on the left.
facilitator.confirmDeleteSession: Delete this training along with all groups and answers?
facilitator.noGroups: Add at least one group to get a link.
facilitator.compareSolo: No other groups in this training yet — comparison will appear once a second team finishes.
facilitator.compareNoDiff: This group's answers are close to the session average.
facilitator.contentLanguageHint: Participants who open a group link will see principles in this language (they can still switch it manually).
facilitator.compareAllLead: Bird's-eye view across all groups: where teams disagreed and where they aligned. Great for projecting during the debrief.
facilitator.mostSplitHint: Biggest gap between max and min 'relevant %' across groups.
facilitator.heatmapHint: Percent who answered 'Relevant' per team. Hover a cell for details.
facilitator.noAnswersYet: Nobody has answered in this session yet.
facilitator.noGroups: Add at least one group to get a link.

start.lead: You are about to see {n} Agile Manifesto principles. Swipe the card right if you find the principle still relevant, or left if you think it is outdated.
start.step3: After each answer you see what your team voted
start.resume: You already answered {n} of {total}. We'll continue where you stopped.

rewrite.title: Rewrite the principle in your own words
rewrite.placeholder: How would you phrase this principle today?

resultsScreen.title: Done!
resultsScreen.controversialHint: Green — 'relevant', red — 'outdated'. The closer to 50/50, the hotter the debate.
resultsScreen.obviousHint: Principles your team agreed on quickly.
resultsScreen.compare: How we differ from other groups
resultsScreen.compareSolo: Only our group has played so far — comparison will appear after other teams finish.
resultsScreen.compareNoDiff: Our answers are close to the average of other groups.
resultsScreen.suggestIntro: Expand any principle and suggest how you would phrase it today. Everyone in your team will see the suggestions.
resultsScreen.suggestNeedStart: To suggest wording, go back to the start screen and begin the training.

error.title: Could not open the training
error.notFound: Group not found. Check the link with your facilitator.

play.languageHint: Language chosen by the facilitator. You can switch it.

cynefin.facSubtitle: Create a training, add teams and share links. Answers are collected separately for each group.
cynefin.mostSplitHint: Biggest gap between max and min 'percent for the expert domain' across groups.
cynefin.mostAlignedHint: Cases where teams aligned on the domain.
cynefin.heatmapHint: Percent of answers matching the expert domain per team.

domainShort.obvious: Cause → effect is clear. Best practices.
domainShort.complicated: Causes exist but need experts and analysis.
domainShort.complex: The system's behaviour only emerges through action.

play.startLead: You are going to work through a series of cases. For each one you'll place it into a Cynefin domain, pick a strategy — and then see what your team thinks.
play.how1: Read the situation and choose a domain
play.how3: See the expert debrief and the team's view
play.dropHint: Drag the card into a domain or click the one you pick.
play.strategyLead: Choose one of the typical approaches for this domain or describe your own.
play.customStrategyPlaceholder: Describe how you would act in this situation…
play.topControversialHint: Cases where answers are split most evenly across domains — perfect for the debrief.
play.noControversy: Too few answers to score controversy yet.

iceberg.facSubtitle: Run a systems-thinking training: create a session, add teams, share the links. You'll see progress, average depth score and cross-team comparison.
iceberg.primaryLevelHint: For each answer we take the deepest level where the participant wrote an intervention.
iceberg.mostSplitHint: Cases with the biggest spread of average depth score between teams.

play.startLead: You'll unpack a few real situations through the iceberg model — from events down to mental models — and propose systemic interventions.
play.how1: Read the case and place facts onto iceberg levels.
play.how2: Build a causal chain: event → pattern → structure → mental model.
play.how3: Tell superficial explanations from real causes.
play.how4: Propose interventions at each level and see the expert take.
play.icebergTitle: Place the facts across iceberg levels
play.icebergHint: Drag cards into the right level, or click a card to pick the level.
play.customHint: Optional — add your own fact or observation.
play.chainHint: Pick one item per level to build a chain 'what → because of → why'.
play.thinkingHint: Mark which of these are symptoms and which are real causes.
play.interventionsHint: Write 1–2 sentences per level: what to do and why.
play.consequencesEventsOnly: If you only solve at the events level
play.consequencesSystem: If you work with the system
play.groupStatsHint: too few answers yet | based on {n} answer | based on {n} answers
play.noTendency: Not enough data to determine your tendency.

tendency.events: You most often propose event-level fixes — quick actions. Useful 'right now', but rarely cures the cause.
tendency.patterns: You notice recurring patterns — a good step toward systems thinking.
tendency.structures: You work at the level of processes and structure — that's where lasting change happens.
tendency.mental_models: You go for mental models — the deepest and slowest, but the most powerful lever.

strategyBuilder.title: Vision, mission and strategy
strategyBuilder.subtitle: Build the strategic frame for your company, department or team
strategyBuilder.lead: A tool for owners and leaders to articulate 'where we are going' — from vision and mission to strategic bets and OKRs. AI suggests wording and examples give you a quick starting point in two languages.
strategyBuilder.bullet1: Pick the scope — company, department or team — AI takes the context into account.
strategyBuilder.bullet2: Any section can be written manually or extended by AI.
strategyBuilder.bullet3: A full example loads in one click — adapt it to your reality.
strategyBuilder.industryPlaceholder: E.g. retail fintech, industrial manufacturing, HR SaaS…
strategyBuilder.aiFailed: Could not get an AI suggestion. Please try again.

vision.title: Vision
vision.hint: A short, vivid picture of the future in 3–5 years. One sentence.

mission.title: Mission
mission.hint: What we do today, for whom, and the change we deliver.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.hint: The deeper 'why' — what we believe in.

values.title: Values
values.hint: 3–5 short values, 1–3 words each. No generic filler — only what actually drives decisions.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.hint: Key pillars, bets for the next year, and success metrics.

okrs.title: Quarterly OKRs
okrs.hint: 2–3 objectives, each with 2–3 measurable Key Results. Focus on change for the customer.

interviewSimulator.voiceUnsupported: Voice input is not supported in this browser.
interviewSimulator.readQuestionAutoHint: Uses your browser voice. For more natural speech, use Chrome/Edge with a neural voice if available.
interviewSimulator.homeLead: Practice a structured technical interview for your role and level. Optionally paste a real job description so questions and feedback align with that vacancy.
interviewSimulator.step1: Pick role and seniority (and optional job description).
interviewSimulator.step3: Receive a detailed report: strengths, gaps, and vacancy fit.
interviewSimulator.voiceSectionBody: You can dictate answers and have questions read aloud (browser support required; HTTPS recommended).
interviewSimulator.serverMockHint: Server is running in mock mode (no OpenAI key or INTERVIEW_SIMULATOR_MOCK=1).
interviewSimulator.sessionReportFailed: Could not generate the final report.
interviewSimulator.noActiveInterview: No active interview. Start from setup.
interviewSimulator.confirmLeave: Leave interview? Unsaved progress will be lost.
interviewSimulator.noReportYet: No report yet. Complete an interview first.
interviewSimulator.jdHint: Paste requirements, stack, and responsibilities. The AI will anchor questions and scoring to this text.
interviewSimulator.jdPlaceholder: Example: We need a Middle Frontend engineer with React, TypeScript, testing (Jest/RTL), REST…

qa.title: Practice: Manual Testing
qa.subtitle: QA course. Find bugs in forms and interfaces — like in a real interview task.
qa.linkHint: This section is available by link only. Save it:
qa.puzzleTriangleDesc: Enter sides and the triangle is drawn. Some cases draw wrong or don't draw. Find all 12 bugs.
qa.puzzleShopDesc: Catalog with filter, search, cart: add, quantity, remove, total. Find 10 bugs.
qa.puzzleTestingTypesDesc: Assignment 3: define testing types for Growboard (Survey, Dashboard). AI scores 1–5; after 5 attempts shows reference.
qa.puzzleUsabilityReportDesc: Assignment 4: fill in the usability test report template. Hints in Russian, AI help per section, PDF export.
qa.puzzleUserStoryDesc: Assignment 5: describe requirements as a user story and acceptance criteria. AI scores quality (1–10). At score ≥8 and ≥10 criteria you can send to development; items appear in the pool on this page.
qa.puzzleTestPlanDesc: Assignment 6: build a test plan for GrowBoard, get AI help, review quality scores, and export to PDF.
qa.puzzleTestCaseDesc: Assignment 7: create a test case from the template, request AI assistance, review quality scores, and export to PDF.
qa.triangleIntro: Enter sides A, B, C. The triangle is drawn below. Try zero, negative, invalid inequality, equilateral, large/small numbers, vertex and side labels. Find 12 drawing bugs.
qa.bugFound: You found a bug! The expected behavior was different.
qa.shopIntro: Filter, search, cart: add, quantity, remove, total. All should work correctly. There are 10 bugs in the code.
qa.shopBug4: Add to cart: same product adds new lines (noticeable with 3+ same)
qa.shopBug5: Remove: deleting first item removes the wrong one
qa.shopBug7: Total recalculates wrong when quantity changes
qa.shopBug8: Total: wrong sum (last item counted as 1)
qa.shopBug10: Cart badge shows number of lines, not sum of quantities
qa.shopBug11: Headphones price shown without decimals (39 instead of 39.9)
qa.shopBug12: Filter "Books": one product from another category appears in list
qa.shopBug14: Add to cart on 2nd product in list adds the 1st product
qa.shopBug15: Removing second item removes the first
qa.shopBug16: Negative quantity is included in total
qa.shopBug17: Total is rounded to integer (loses cents)
qa.shopBug18: In cart, Headphones show Smartphone name
qa.shopBug19: Cart badge shows one more than actual line count
qa.shopBug20: Filter "Books" with empty search shows "No products"
qa.triangleBug1: Zero side still draws a triangle
qa.triangleBug9: Vertex labels A and B swapped (3,4,5)
qa.task1Title: Find the bug in the text
qa.task1Desc: One of the first tasks of a tester is to check texts for typos and errors.
qa.task2Desc: Think about what you would check on a login form.
qa.task3Desc: A good bug report helps the developer find and fix the issue quickly.
qa.task4Desc: Prioritizing tests is an important QA skill.
qa.task5Desc: A short quiz about the QA role on the team.
qa.findBugInstruction: There is a typo (one wrong word) in the text below. Find it and click on it.
qa.findBugTextBefore: On the login page the user enters username and password. After clicking «Login» the dashboard should open. If the data is wrong, an
qa.findBugSuccess: Correct! «Erorr» is a typo; it should be «error». Testers catch these when checking copy.
qa.checklistInstruction: Imagine you need to test a «Login» form. Check what you would verify first.
qa.checklistHint: All items matter. The key is to think like a tester: what could break?
qa.bugReportInstruction: Scenario: the «Submit» button does not respond to clicks. Which bug description is best for the developer?
qa.bugReportSuccess: Correct! A good bug report has steps to reproduce, expected and actual result — so the developer can fix it faster.
qa.bugReportHintWrong: Hint: the developer needs steps to reproduce, expected and actual result. Pick the option that has all of these.
qa.priorityInstruction: A new «Download report» button was added. What would you test first?
qa.priorityHint: First we check the main thing: the button does what it’s supposed to. Then layout and edge cases.
qa.quizQuestion: Who is a tester on a development team?
qa.quizSuccess: Correct! A tester helps find issues before users see them.
qa.footer: Done with all tasks? See you at the second lecture — more to come!
qa.checklist2: Wrong password shows an error message
qa.checklist3: Login and password fields can be filled
qa.checklist4: After successful login, the dashboard opens
qa.bugReportB: Steps: 1) Fill the form 2) Click «Submit». Expected: data is sent. Actual: nothing happens.
qa.priorityC: Button is visible only to admins
qa.quizB: A specialist who checks the product and finds bugs before release
qa.quizC: The person who brings coffee to the team
qa.userStoryTaskTitle: Assignment 5: User story and acceptance criteria
qa.userStoryTaskIntro: Describe a requirement as a user story and list acceptance criteria (one per line). AI will score quality 1–10. At score ≥8 and ≥10 criteria the «Send to development» button unlocks. Submitted items appear in the pool below — everyone can view, edit, and delete.
qa.userStoryPlaceholder: As a <role>, I want <action> so that <value>
qa.userStoryDeleteConfirm: Delete this item from the pool?
qa.puzzleSqlDesc: Assignment 8: 16 SQL lessons, e-commerce demo data (customers, products, orders, line items), in-browser task checks.
qa.puzzleAutoTestsDesc: Run Growboard API checks from the browser (sign-in required).
qa.sqlPageLead: 16 lessons on the left; each has theory and several checked tasks. On the right: customers, products, orders, and order_items (JOIN-friendly), editor, and the last SELECT result.
qa.sqlBrowserOnly: Runs only on your device (SQLite in the browser); SQL is not sent to the GrowBoard server.
qa.sqlCorrectAnswerExplainer: One valid query over the same data. If the task does not mention sorting or a “top N”, the reference has no ORDER BY—do not add it yourself. ORDER BY and LIMIT are required only where the task says so (lesson 5 and top-N tasks). Row order is ignored; other column names and SELECT * are fine if the column set matches (see the tasks hint).
qa.sqlDemoTablesHint: Tables: customers, products, orders, order_items. For tasks, prefer SELECT; Reset database restores demo data.
qa.sqlEditorHint: You can use multiple statements separated by semicolons; the result panel shows the last column set from a successful run (usually the last SELECT).
qa.sqlAutoRunHint: Queries run automatically ~0.5s after you stop typing. Use the button below to run immediately.
qa.sqlResultEmpty: Click Run to see the result table.
qa.sqlNoSelectResult: Executed. No row set to display (e.g. only INSERT/UPDATE or empty SELECT).
qa.sqlTasksHint: Your query is checked automatically after a typing pause (or use Run now). Tasks are phrased as information needs (“what you want to know from the data”); you choose the SQL yourself. Grading matches the reference result set (row order ignored; column names must match the reference where specified; SELECT * is OK if every required column is present). After a correct answer your SQL stays in the editor — pick the next task by number on the left.
qa.sqlCorrectCongrats: Congratulations! You entered the correct query.
qa.sqlWrongNoSelect: Your query should return a table (last successful SELECT). Check syntax and conditions.
qa.sqlWrongResult: Result does not match the expected data: row multiset and values in the required columns must match (extra columns are allowed if names match the reference; row order is ignored).
qa.sqlLessonDoneBody: You finished all tasks in this lesson. Go to the next one?
qa.sqlCourseCompleteBody: You finished all lessons. You can redo tasks anytime; to reset progress, clear site data for this app.

backlogPrep.title: Backlog Prep
backlogPrep.subtitle: Sharpen a user story or epic before planning: the AI highlights gaps and suggests improvements.
backlogPrep.introLead: Like the systems-thinking iceberg digs into causes, this tool deepens the requirement: one vague line is not enough — you need acceptance criteria, context, and clarity for the team.
backlogPrep.introExpand: Show how to use the form
backlogPrep.introStep1Body: User stories are smaller increments; epics bundle larger value. Response language affects how the AI phrases analysis output.
backlogPrep.introStep2Body: Use “As a … I want … so that …”, plus dedicated fields for acceptance criteria and non-functional requirements. Use AI assist from a short note for a first draft.
backlogPrep.introStep3Body: Team, stakeholders, deadlines, dependencies, risks — anything that affects prioritization and estimation.
backlogPrep.assistHint: Type a free-form idea — the AI suggests a fuller description and context block. Existing text is merged and improved when useful.
backlogPrep.assistRun: AI: fill description, context, AC & NFR
backlogPrep.assistNeedHint: Add a note or at least a draft in Description.
backlogPrep.hintWorkType: Story: one deliverable; epic: a large theme to split later.
backlogPrep.hintLanguage: Language the AI uses for analysis output (questions, gaps, example).
backlogPrep.hintDescription: Main requirement text: role, action, value; add acceptance criteria when possible.
backlogPrep.hintContext: Business goal, constraints, systems, sponsor — for a practical review.
backlogPrep.descriptionPlaceholder: As a user I want ... so that ...
backlogPrep.contextPlaceholder: Team, business goal, constraints, risks, etc.
backlogPrep.libraryLead: Saved epics and stories — edit, delete, or copy in Jira-friendly formats.
backlogPrep.libraryEmpty: Nothing saved yet — use “Save to library” or create an epic from a specification.
backlogPrep.deleteConfirm: Delete this item? Deleting an epic also removes its child stories.
backlogPrep.specLead: Upload .txt / .md / .csv / .docx or paste text (80+ chars). AI proposes an epic and story decomposition.
backlogPrep.specTooShort: Add a file or at least 80 characters of text.
backlogPrep.hintTitle: Short, like a work item title; if empty, the first line of the description is used.
backlogPrep.hintAc: One per line or bullets; included in the AC block when copying to Jira.
backlogPrep.hintParentEpic: Link a story to a saved epic when creating or updating.
backlogPrep.blockDecomposeLead: Upload or paste a business spec — the AI suggests an epic and candidate user stories.
backlogPrep.blockComposeLead: Fill fields manually or with AI assist, save to your library, and optionally link a story to an epic.
backlogPrep.blockReviewLead: After “Check description”, you get gaps, questions, ideas, and a structured improved draft (description, context, 10+ acceptance criteria, NFR). Epics can be decomposed into stories.
backlogPrep.blockReviewEmpty: Run “Check description” to analyze your draft. Results will show in this panel.
backlogPrep.appliedImprovedToForm: Form updated from the improved draft

tests.title: Portal Auto Tests
tests.hint: Run all tests or pick a category. Failed test details can be copied for debugging.

metricsTree.subtitle: PROFIT → Revenue/Costs → delivery and team metrics
metricsTree.searchPlaceholder: Search a metric (e.g. Lead Time, MTTR, Velocity)
